market_baseline: Defensive — SPY +0.46% overnight as CPI meets expectations, but VIX remains elevated (25.01) and $100 Brent crude oil surge threatens to derail March 18 Fed rate cut hopes.

ai_word_of_the_day.definition: An AI architecture that divides tasks among multiple specialized sub-networks ('experts'), using a 'gating network' to activate only the most relevant experts for a given input, significantly improving efficiency at scale.

items.source_title: Bitcoin Surges to $71K Following Trump Middle East Remarks
items.content: Bitcoin surged to $71,100 on March 13 following social media dissemination of comments from Donald Trump regarding the Middle East conflict. The asset remains volatile as geopolitical tensions drive 'digital gold' demand. Impact: High volatility for crypto-adjacent tickers like BITU.

items.source_title: IEA Announces Record 400M Barrel Oil Release to Combat Surging Energy Costs
items.content: The International Energy Agency (IEA) approved a record 400 million-barrel emergency release of oil reserves on March 11-13 as Brent crude approaches $100. Markets expect the Trump administration to suspend the Jones Act to further lower transportation costs. Impact: Inflationary pressure persists despite liquidity injection.

items.source_title: OpenAI to Integrate Sora Video Generation into ChatGPT, Launches Codex-Spark
items.content: OpenAI plans to natively bake Sora video generation into ChatGPT. Simultaneously, the release of GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark, a lightweight variant for coding, signals a strategic move to reduce reliance on NVIDIA for inference workloads.

items.source_title: NVIDIA Commits $26B to Transform into Full-Stack AI Laboratory
items.content: NVIDIA is pivoting from a chipmaker to a 'full-stack AI laboratory,' committing $26B over five years for open-weighted models and the NemoClaw platform. Adoption of agentic AI has hit 50% in telecom/retail sectors as of March 2026.

items.source_title: Spain Fines Booking.com Record €413M for Anti-Competitive Abuse
items.content: Spain's CNMC watchdog imposed a landmark €413 million fine on Booking.com on March 12 for abusing its dominant market position. The ruling alleges the platform's clauses suppressed competition from other OTAs and harmed hotel pricing independence.

items.source_title: Marriott AI Platform Boosts Revenue 50%; Named FIFA World Cup Support
items.content: Marriott's AI-powered 'Personalized Experience Platform' has successfully driven a 50% increase in ancillary revenue. The company also announced a partnership as an Official Hotel Supporter for the FIFA World Cup 2026, targeting massive sports tourism demand.

items.source_title: UK CMA Investigates Hilton and Marriott for Alleged Data-Sharing Collusion
items.content: The UK Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) has opened an investigation into Hilton, Marriott, and IHG for suspected anticompetitive data-sharing practices. The probe focuses on whether the firms exchanged sensitive pricing and occupancy data.
